item.title: CV
item.description: Curriculum Vitae (Updated May 2026)

item.title: Teaching
item.description:
A list of courses I have taught or assisted with.

PUBPOL825 Health Policy
Level: Graduate | Term: 2026 Fall
Role: Teaching assistant
Instructor: Kate Bundolf
Institution: Sanford School of Public Policy, Duke University

PUBPOL812 Statistics for Public Policy Makers
Level: Graduate | Term: 2024 Fall
Role: Teaching assistant leading weekly lab sessions, code available here.
Instructor: Marcos Rangel
Institution: Sanford School of Public Policy, Duke University

PUBPOL304 Economics of The Public Sector
Level: Undergrad | Term: 2022 Fall
Role: Teaching assistant
Instructor: Sara A. Sutherland
Institution: Sanford School of Public Policy, Duke University

item.title: Research
item.description:
A map of the main strands of my research work.

Wealth Trajectories

→ Qiu Shuyi. “The Long Arm of Childhood on Trajectory of Wealth Accumulation and Allocation Patterns in Early and Middle Adulthood” (Under review)

→ Qiu Shuyi. “The Long-term Impact of the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) Exposure in Childhood on Individual’s Wealth Trajectory in Adulthood” (Manuscripts Available Upon Request)

→ Keister, Lisa A., James Moody, Shuyi Qiu. (2026). “Rethinking Financial Status: A Comprehensive Case-Based Approach” Social Currents Forthcoming [DOI] [PrePrint]
